description: Navigation/metadata manifest for the Seelen UI documentation viewer. Markdown files remain the source of truth for content; this file only describes structure, ordering and status. TOC/headings should be extracted from each file at build time, not duplicated here.

docs.title: Resource Guidelines
docs.description: Shared concepts for all resource kinds: resource IDs, metadata, i18n, extended YAML (!include/!extend), folder structure, loading/bundling with the slu CLI.

docs.title: ResourceText & Translations
docs.description: The ResourceText type used for every user-facing string in a resource, and the slu resource translate workflow.

docs.title: Supported Languages
docs.description: Where the canonical language list lives and how it drives the language picker and translation tooling.

docs.title: Theme Guidelines
docs.description: How to create a theme: per-widget styles, shared styles, CSS variable settings, system CSS variables.

docs.title: Widget Guidelines
docs.description: How to build a custom widget: metadata.yml, presets/instances, settings schema, CSS conventions, plugin extensibility.

docs.title: Widget JS API
docs.description: The @seelen-ui/lib runtime API: Widget.self.init()/.ready() lifecycle, the invoke/subscribe wrappers, and where commands/events are actually declared (Rust) and generated (TS).

docs.title: Plugin Guidelines
docs.description: What a plugin is: a flat declaration file (id/target/plugin) with no schema or runtime of its own — each target widget defines how to parse and execute it. Start here before the per-widget plugin guides.

docs.title: Toolbar Plugins
docs.description: The @seelen/fancy-toolbar plugin schema: scopes, template/tooltip/badge/onClick scripts, remoteData, and the sandboxed execution model.

docs.title: Dock Plugins
docs.description: The @seelen/weg (dock) plugin schema: render/tooltip/badge/onClick, canvas vs. icon-key rendering, the dock's own whitelisted commands, and a full fictitious example loaded via the slu CLI.

docs.title: Window Manager Layouts
docs.description: The @seelen/window-manager plugin schema: the declarative TwmPluginNode tree (Leaf/Stack/Vertical/Horizontal), growFactor, condition expressions, and stack policies.
